transform.h File Reference

Public API for the Transform C library. More...

#include <stdbool.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include "media-c/stream_buffers.h"
#include "media.h"
#include "transform-c/version.h"

 Transform C API handle, extending the base media handle.

 Transition from Initialized back to Null.

 Query which input streams a given output stream depends on.

 Query compatible output capabilities given dependent input constraints.
 
static void transform_inspect (const transform_t *self, int(*print_func)(const char *,...))
 Print a human-readable dump of the transform element.
 

Detailed Description

Author
IMD Software Team
Attention

Copyright (c) 2026 STMicroelectronics. All rights reserved.

This software is licensed under terms that can be found in the LICENSE file in the root directory of this software component. If no LICENSE file comes with this software, it is provided AS-IS.

The Transform C API extends the base Media C API with stream-processing capabilities. A transform_t handle embeds a media_t as its first member, allowing direct reuse of all media lifecycle, control and stream operations. On top of that the Transform API adds:

  • Stream dependency queries – determine which input streams an output stream depends on.
  • Compatible capability queries – negotiate output capabilities given constraints from a dependent input stream.
  • Stream processing – push/pull data through the transform.

All functions in this header are thin inline wrappers that delegate to the underlying media handle or to the transform-specific virtual function pointers.

Lifecycle Management

transform_get_state — Query the current lifecycle state.

transform_initialize — Transition from MEDIA_STATE_NULL to MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED. Acquires hardware handles and internal resources.

transform_prepare — Transition from MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED to MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED. Validates and applies static controls, allocates resources that depend on the static configuration.

transform_finalize — Transition from MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED back to MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED. Releases resources allocated during preparation.

transform_release — Transition from MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED back to MEDIA_STATE_NULL. Frees all resources acquired during initialization.

Information & Inspection

transform_about — Retrieve element metadata (name, description, version, license, URL, open-source dependencies).

transform_get_version — Get the implemented Transform API version (major, minor, patch).

transform_inspect — Print a human-readable dump of the element (about, streams, controls) via a printf-like callback.

Streams & Capabilities

transform_get_streams — List all input/output streams.

transform_get_stream_capabilities — Get the current capabilities of a named stream.

transform_set_stream_capabilities — Set desired capabilities on a named stream.

transform_query_stream_dependencies — Query which input streams a given output stream depends on.

transform_query_compatible_stream_caps — Query compatible output capabilities given constraints from a dependent input stream.

Controls

transform_get_controls — List all available controls (static and dynamic).

transform_get_control — Read the current value of a control by name. Can be called at any time.

transform_set_control — Write a new value to a control by name. Static controls can only be set before transform_prepare; dynamic controls can be changed at any time.

Processing

transform_process_stream — Process one iteration of stream buffers through the transform.

Function Documentation

◆ transform_about()

static int transform_about ( const transform_t * self,
const properties_t ** properties )
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_about() on the embedded media handle. Returns properties such as name, description, version, license, URL, and open-source dependencies.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Ownership of the returned properties is not transferred to the caller; do not free them.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[out]propertiesPointer to receive the properties_t.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or properties is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.

◆ transform_finalize()

static int transform_finalize ( transform_t * self)
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_finalize() on the embedded media handle. Tears down everything that was allocated during transform_prepare(): compiled GPU kernels, look-up tables, calibration data, etc.

After this call, static controls can be changed again and the instance can be re-prepared with a subsequent call to transform_prepare().

Parameters
[in,out]selfPointer to the transform instance.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED.
Postcondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED on success; unchanged on failure.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEInstance is not in MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
Note
This function may block while the implementation releases GPU resources or waits for pending operations to complete.
See also
transform_prepare(), transform_release()

◆ transform_get_control()

static int transform_get_control ( const transform_t * self,
const char *const name,
value_t * value )
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_get_control() on the embedded media handle. Controls can be read as soon as a transform instance is available, regardless of the lifecycle state.

Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[in]nameName of the control (must match a name from transform_get_controls()).
[out]valuePointer to a value_t that receives the current control value.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or value is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_NOT_FOUNDNo control with name name exists.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_set_control(), transform_get_controls()

◆ transform_get_controls()

static int transform_get_controls ( const transform_t * self,
const controls_t ** controls )
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_get_controls() on the embedded media handle. Controls may be static (cannot be changed after transform_prepare()) or dynamic (can be changed at any time).

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Ownership of the returned list is not transferred to the caller; do not free it.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[out]controlsPointer to receive the controls_t list.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or controls is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_get_control(), transform_set_control()

◆ transform_get_state()

static int transform_get_state ( const transform_t * self,
media_state_t * state )
inlinestatic

Returns the current state of the transform element.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[out]statePointer to receive the current media_state_t value.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or state is NULL.
See also
transform_initialize(), transform_prepare(), transform_finalize(), transform_release()

◆ transform_get_stream_capabilities()

static int transform_get_stream_capabilities ( const transform_t * self,
const char *const name,
const capabilities_t ** caps )
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_get_stream_capabilities() on the embedded media handle.

Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[in]nameName of the stream (must match a name from transform_get_streams()).
[out]capsPointer to receive the capabilities_t. Ownership is not transferred to the caller.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or caps is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_set_stream_capabilities(), transform_get_streams()

◆ transform_get_streams()

static int transform_get_streams ( const transform_t * self,
const streams_t ** streams )
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_get_streams() on the embedded media handle. Streams are the input and output interfaces of the transform element.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Ownership of the returned list is not transferred to the caller; do not free it.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[out]streamsPointer to receive the streams_t list.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or streams is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_get_stream_capabilities(), transform_set_stream_capabilities()

◆ transform_get_version()

static void transform_get_version ( const transform_t * self,
uint32_t * major,
uint32_t * minor,
uint32_t * patch )
inlinestatic

Returns the compile-time version constants defined in version.h.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[out]majorPointer to receive the major version number.
[out]minorPointer to receive the minor version number.
[out]patchPointer to receive the patch version number.

◆ transform_initialize()

static int transform_initialize ( transform_t * self)
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_initialize() on the embedded media handle. Acquires hardware handles, internal bookkeeping structures, and any resources needed for the transform to operate.

Parameters
[in,out]selfPointer to the transform instance.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_NULL.
Postcondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED on success; unchanged on failure.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEInstance is not in MEDIA_STATE_NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDOne or more virtual functions are not set.
See also
transform_release(), transform_prepare()

◆ transform_inspect()

static void transform_inspect ( const transform_t * self,
int(* print_func )(const char *,...) )
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_inspect() on the embedded media handle. Writes a summary including the about properties, stream list, and control list.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[in]print_funcPrintf-like callback used for output.
See also
transform_about(), transform_get_streams(), transform_get_controls()

◆ transform_prepare()

static int transform_prepare ( transform_t * self)
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_prepare() on the embedded media handle. Validates and applies static controls, then allocates resources that depend on the static configuration (kernel programs, look-up tables, calibration data, …).

All static controls must be set via transform_set_control() before calling this function.

Parameters
[in,out]selfPointer to the transform instance.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED.
All mandatory static controls have been set with valid values.
Postcondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED on success; unchanged on failure.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEInstance is not in MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
Note
This function may block while the implementation compiles GPU kernels, loads calibration files, or performs other heavyweight preparation.
See also
transform_finalize(), transform_set_control()

◆ transform_process_stream()

static int transform_process_stream ( const transform_t * self,
const stream_buffers_t * stream_buffers )
inlinestatic

Pushes input stream buffers into the transform and retrieves processed output buffers. Some output streams may depend on multiple input streams; their processing is deferred until all dependencies are satisfied.

Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[in]stream_buffersCollection of named stream_buffers_t.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED or MEDIA_STATE_STREAMING.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or stream_buffers is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_prepare()

◆ transform_query_compatible_stream_caps()

static int transform_query_compatible_stream_caps ( const transform_t * self,
const char * name,
const char * dependent_stream_name,
const capabilities_t * dependent_stream_caps,
capabilities_t ** compatible_caps )
inlinestatic

Given the capabilities of a dependent (input) stream, returns the set of output capabilities that are compatible with those constraints.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[in]nameName of the stream to query.
[in]dependent_stream_nameName of the dependent stream providing constraints.
[in]dependent_stream_capsCapabilities of the dependent stream.
[out]compatible_capsPointer to receive the compatible capabilities_t.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERAny argument is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_query_stream_dependencies(), transform_get_stream_capabilities()

◆ transform_query_stream_dependencies()

static int transform_query_stream_dependencies ( const transform_t * self,
const char * name,
const strings_t ** dependencies )
inlinestatic

Returns the list of input stream names that the specified output stream depends on for its processing.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Ownership of the returned list is not transferred to the caller; do not free it.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[in]nameName of the stream to query.
[out]dependenciesPointer to receive the strings_t list of dependent stream names.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or dependencies is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_query_compatible_stream_caps(), transform_get_streams()

◆ transform_release()

static int transform_release ( transform_t * self)
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_release() on the embedded media handle. Frees every resource acquired during transform_initialize().

After this call the instance returns to its initial state and can be re-initialized with a new call to transform_initialize().

Parameters
[in,out]selfPointer to the transform instance.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED (transform_finalize() must have been called first if the instance was previously prepared).
Postcondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_NULL on success; unchanged on failure.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEInstance is not in MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_initialize(), transform_finalize()

◆ transform_set_control()

static int transform_set_control ( const transform_t * self,
const char * name,
const value_t value )
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_set_control() on the embedded media handle. Controls can be set as soon as a transform instance is available.

  • Static controls (CTRL_FLAGS_STATIC) — can be set at any time before transform_prepare() is called. Attempting to change a static control once the instance is in MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED or later returns MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATE.
  • Dynamic controls (CTRL_FLAGS_DYNAMIC) — can be changed at any time, regardless of the current state.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[in]nameName of the control (must match a name from transform_get_controls()).
[in]valueThe value_t to assign.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or name is NULL, or value has an invalid type.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEname is a static control and the instance is already in MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED or later.
MEDIA_ERROR_NOT_FOUNDNo control with name name exists.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_get_control(), transform_get_controls(), transform_prepare()

◆ transform_set_stream_capabilities()

static int transform_set_stream_capabilities ( const transform_t * self,
const char *const name,
const capabilities_t * caps )
inlinestatic

Delegates to media_set_stream_capabilities() on the embedded media handle. The implementation validates that the requested capabilities are compatible with the current configuration.

Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the transform instance.
[in]nameName of the stream (must match a name from transform_get_streams()).
[in]capsPointer to the capabilities_t to apply.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or caps is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
transform_get_stream_capabilities(), transform_query_compatible_stream_caps()
Generated by  1.12.0
